query: CIA Director John Ratcliffe visit to Cuba May 2026 details and any follow-up visits by US cabinet officials planned
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Key Findings

**The Ratcliffe Visit (May 14–15, 2026):**
CIA Director John Ratcliffe led a US delegation to Havana on **Thursday, May 14, 2026** — one of the highest-level US-Cuba engagements in years. He met at the Interior Ministry with **Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro ("El Cangrejo")** (Raúl Castro's grandson), Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas, and senior intelligence officials. He delivered a message from Trump that the US would expand engagement only if Cuba makes "fundamental changes." Cuba officially confirmed the visit. It was widely described as only the second CIA director visit to Cuba (after Brennan in 2015).

**On follow-up cabinet-official visits — the key answer:**
- **No US cabinet-secretary-level visit to Cuba has been publicly announced or scheduled** as a follow-up (as of late June/July 2026).
- Secretary of State **Marco Rubio** did *not* travel to Cuba but commented repeatedly (May 21: "no substantive progress"; May 27 cabinet meeting: "We will talk to them, we will work on this").
- The **clearest confirmed follow-up in-person engagement** was **SOUTHCOM commander Gen. Francis Donovan** meeting senior Cuban military officials at the **perimeter of Guantanamo Bay (~May 29–30, 2026)**.
- By **June 30, 2026**, Cuban FM Bruno Rodríguez said talks showed "no progress" despite Cuba's June market-opening reforms; the US responded with new sanctions. Cuba scheduled a **July 7 UN General Assembly session** on the blockade.

The trajectory points toward stalemate rather than a scheduled next round of senior visits. Full synthesis and structured data (sources, data points, key facts) have been delivered.

key_facts: CIA Director John Ratcliffe visited Havana, Cuba on Thursday May 14, 2026 (reported May 14-15), leading a US delegation.

He met with Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro ('El Cangrejo', grandson of Raúl Castro), Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas, and senior Cuban intelligence officials at the Ministry of Interior; US Ambassador Mike Hammer was also present.
Ratcliffe delivered a message from Trump that the US would expand economic/security engagement only if Cuba makes 'fundamental changes.'
The Cuban government officially confirmed the visit, stating the US requested it and it was approved by 'the Direction of the Revolution.'
Widely described as only the second CIA director visit to Cuba (after John Brennan in 2015); first in such a confrontational context since the revolution.

The visit occurred amid a severe energy crisis driven by a US fuel blockade begun February 2026 and secondary sanctions in early May 2026; Cuba announced May 14 it had run out of oil/diesel with 15-24 hour blackouts.
No US cabinet-secretary-level visit to Cuba has been publicly announced or scheduled as a follow-up as of late June/July 2026.
Secretary of State Marco Rubio did NOT travel to Cuba but commented on talks: May 21 ('no substantive progress', agreement unlikely) and May 27 ('We will talk to them, we will work on this').
The clearest confirmed follow-up official engagement was SOUTHCOM commander Gen. Francis Donovan meeting senior Cuban military officials at the perimeter of Guantanamo Bay around May 29-30, 2026.
By June 30, 2026, Cuban FM Bruno Rodríguez said US-Cuba talks showed 'no progress' despite Cuba's June market-opening reforms; US responded with new sanctions on Cuban firms.
Cuba scheduled a UN General Assembly session on July 7, 2026 to denounce the US energy blockade.

Backchannel/negotiation contacts had run since February 2026, with reported meetings around April 10 and April 17 in Havana; the Trump administration was reportedly developing military action plans against Cuba (reported May 20).
